note: Idea pool: the New Ideas button draws from here. The daily agent refills it using signals + weights.

items.title: The $18,000 year
items.hook: I paid Thumbtack $18,000 last year. For leads. That maybe called back.
items.source_signal: Pain bank: vendors report $1,500-2,000/month in credits at ~20% conversion
items.notes: Whiteboard or receipt-stack visual. End card: what $0 in fees looks like.

items.title: That's not a lead, that's a lottery
items.hook: Angi sold my lead to 5 other contractors. We're all calling the same lady.
items.source_signal: Pain bank quote, word for word. The complaint content on TikTok has no brand answering it.
items.notes: Skit: 5 phones ringing on one kitchen counter. Homeowner overwhelmed. Cut to direct chat on Gozal.

items.title: Day one on Gozal
items.hook: First morning without a lead fee. Here's what happened.
items.source_signal: Vendor activation research: first-transaction stories drive signups
items.notes: Real vendor, phone footage, first direct customer message on screen. Yotam films.

items.title: Tony's math
items.hook: 20% of your leads convert. You pay for 100%. Slide the math.
items.source_signal: Pain bank conversion complaints; carousel = save/share format
items.notes: Slide-by-slide cost breakdown, Gozal yellow on black. Final slide: keep 100%.

items.title: Why I left (vendor testimonial template)
items.hook: Fifteen years in the trade. Two years paying to compete against fake reviews. Done.
items.source_signal: Pain bank: fake review frustration; testimonial format humanizes supply side
items.notes: Template for repeatable series: one vendor, one reason, 20 seconds, their own words.

items.title: Callback purgatory
items.hook: Day 3 of waiting for a plumber to call back. He's not calling back.
items.source_signal: Maria ICP: called 3 pros, two never answered, one booked 2 weeks out
items.notes: Montage of waiting by the phone getting more absurd. Resolution: direct chat, plumber tomorrow 9am.

items.title: The service fee reveal
items.hook: The quote said $150. Checkout says $189. What happened?
items.source_signal: Pain bank consumer quote: fees revealed at checkout
items.notes: Split screen: other apps' checkout vs Gozal (same number start to finish).

items.title: The property manager bench
items.hook: She manages 14 properties and stopped scrambling for vendors. Here's her setup.
items.source_signal: Sandra ICP (priority, highest LTV): needs 10 reliable pros across trades
items.notes: Case-study style. Reliability angle, not price angle. Also fits LinkedIn.

items.title: 60-second backup cleaner
items.hook: Guest checks in at 3. Cleaner ghosted at noon. Watch this.
items.source_signal: Carlos ICP panic moment; STR host communities are approved priority targets
items.notes: Screen-record the actual app flow in real time with a visible timer. Authenticity is the pitch.

items.title: DIY or call a pro?
items.hook: 6 home fixes people get wrong, and the one rule for knowing which is which.
items.source_signal: Education pillar earns saves/shares; zero selling builds trust
items.notes: Screenshot-worthy. Gozal branding light, value heavy.

items.title: Vet any contractor in 5 minutes
items.hook: Before you hire anyone: license lookup, insurance ask, and the one question that filters 90% of flakes.
items.source_signal: Neighbor-mode value post; Nextdoor is a standing pillar channel
items.notes: Pure helpfulness. Gozal mentioned once at the end, naturally, no link.

items.title: 5 free things
items.hook: 5 things pros on Gozal give away free that other apps charge them for.
items.source_signal: Ariel's earlier UGC concept, revived: profile, direct chat, reviews, photos, leads
items.notes: Fast-cut listicle, vendor voiceover. Countdown format holds retention.
